title: The Demon-Haunted World
author: Carl Sagan
notes: Science as a candle in the dark. Definitive case for skepticism and critical thinking.

title: Bad Blood
author: John Carreyrou
notes: Inside the Theranos fraud. Best investigative nonfiction of the decade.

title: The New Jim Crow
author: Michelle Alexander
notes: Mass incarceration in the age of colorblindness. Landmark social justice work.

title: The Hard Thing About Hard Things
author: Ben Horowitz
notes: Building a business when there are no easy answers. The most honest startup book ever written.

title: The Black Swan
author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb
notes: The impact of the highly improbable. Changed how people think about risk.

title: How to Change Your Mind
author: Michael Pollan
notes: Psychedelics and consciousness exploration.

title: The Myth of Normal
author: Gabor Maté & Daniel Maté
notes: Trauma, illness, and healing in a toxic culture. Maté's most comprehensive work.

title: Leonardo da Vinci
author: Walter Isaacson
notes: Biography of the ultimate Renaissance man. Isaacson at his best.

title: The Making of the Atomic Bomb
author: Richard Rhodes
notes: Comprehensive, authoritative history. Pulitzer Prize winner.

title: Killing the Rising Sun
author: Bill O'Reilly & Martin Dugard
notes: Popular history of America's victory over Japan in WWII.

title: Unmasking Autism
author: Devon Price
notes: Discovering the new faces of neurodiversity. Validating and thorough.

title: God Is Not Great
author: Christopher Hitchens
notes: Anti-religion polemic.

title: The Third Reich at War
author: Richard J. Evans
notes: The final volume of Evans's landmark trilogy. Hailed a masterpiece by the NYT.

title: Dark Money
author: Jane Mayer
notes: The hidden history of the billionaires behind the radical right.

title: Basic Economics
author: Thomas Sowell
notes: Economics without jargon. The definitive introductory economics text.

title: Cracking the Coding Interview
author: Gayle Laakmann McDowell
notes: 189 programming questions and solutions. Essential prep for technical interviews.

title: The Rise and Fall of the Third Reich
author: William L. Shirer
notes: Classic comprehensive Nazi Germany history.

title: The True Believer
author: Eric Hoffer
notes: Mass movements and fanaticism analysis.

title: Too Big to Fail
author: Andrew Ross Sorkin
notes: Inside the 2008 financial crisis. Best account of how Wall Street nearly collapsed.

title: AI Superpowers
author: Kai-Fu Lee
notes: China, Silicon Valley, and the new world order. Still relevant.

title: The Hundred Years' War on Palestine
author: Rashid Khalidi
notes: History of settler colonialism and resistance, 1917-2017.

title: The Battle of Midway
author: Craig L. Symonds
notes: Masterful account of the pivotal WWII Pacific battle.

title: Phenomenology of Spirit
author: G.W.F. Hegel
notes: Dense philosophy classic.

title: American Prometheus
author: Kai Bird & Martin Sherwin
notes: Oppenheimer biography. Pulitzer Prize winner.

title: Empire of AI
author: Karen Hao
notes: Dreams and nightmares inside Sam Altman's OpenAI. NYT Bestseller.

title: Leviathan
author: Thomas Hobbes
notes: Political philosophy classic (1651).

title: The Thinking Machine
author: Stephen Witt
notes: Jensen Huang, NVIDIA, and the world's most coveted microchip. The definitive Huang biography.

title: The Wright Brothers
author: David McCullough
notes: The story of Orville and Wilbur Wright. Pulitzer Prize-winning historian.

title: The Snowball
author: Alice Schroeder
notes: Warren Buffett biography.

title: Breath
author: James Nestor
notes: The new science of a lost art. Surprisingly compelling.

title: The Power of Geography
author: Tim Marshall
notes: How geography shapes nations.

title: The 33 Strategies of War
author: Robert Greene
notes: Strategic thinking guide.

title: The Making of a Manager
author: Julie Zhuo
notes: What to do when everyone looks to you. Practical management guide.

title: The Infinity Machine
author: Sebastian Mallaby
notes: Demis Hassabis, DeepMind, and the quest for superintelligence. Definitive AI biography.

title: Subliminal
author: Leonard Mlodinow
notes: Unconscious mind science.

title: Hidden Potential
author: Adam Grant
notes: The science of achieving greater things.

title: This Is Your Mind on Plants
author: Michael Pollan
notes: Opium, caffeine, mescaline, our relationship with psychoactive plants.

title: Stop Walking on Eggshells
author: Paul T. Mason & Randi Kreger
notes: Taking your life back when someone you care about has borderline personality disorder.

title: The Ascent of Money
author: Niall Ferguson
notes: A financial history of the world. 10th anniversary edition.

title: Million Dollar Weekend
author: Noah Kagan
notes: The surprisingly simple way to launch a 7-figure business in 48 hours.

title: The Holy Grail of Investing
author: Tony Robbins & Christopher Zook
notes: The world's greatest investors reveal their ultimate strategies.

title: The Inevitable
author: Kevin Kelly
notes: Technology futures.

title: Steve Jobs in Exile
author: Geoffrey Cain
notes: The untold story of NeXT and the remaking of an American visionary.

title: More Everything Forever
author: Adam Becker
notes: AI optimism and the case for abundance.

title: Legacy of Ashes
author: Tim Weiner
notes: The history of the CIA. Pulitzer Prize winner.

title: Big Debt Crises
author: Ray Dalio
notes: Template for understanding debt cycles. Dalio's most rigorous work.

title: AI 2041
author: Kai-Fu Lee & Chen Qiufan
notes: Ten visions for our future. Fiction-nonfiction hybrid on how AI will reshape the world.

title: Palo Alto
author: Malcolm Harris
notes: Silicon Valley history and critique.

title: The Contrarian
author: Max Chafkin
notes: Peter Thiel and the rise of the Silicon Valley oligarchs.

title: Supremacy
author: Parmy Olson
notes: AI, ChatGPT, and the race that will change the world.

title: The Loudest Voice in the Room
author: Gabriel Sherman
notes: Roger Ailes and the rise of Fox News.

title: Enshittification
author: Cory Doctorow
notes: Why everything suddenly got worse and what to do about it.

title: How to Rule the World
author: Theo Baker
notes: An education in power at Stanford University. The culture of ascendancy exposed.

title: The Philosopher in the Valley
author: Michael Steinberger
notes: Alex Karp, Palantir, and the rise of the surveillance state.

title: The Mind-Gut Connection
author: Emeran Mayer
notes: Digestive health and brain connection.

title: The LEGO Story
author: Jens Andersen
notes: Behind the brick empire. Family drama and business reinvention.

title: Bonfire of the Murdochs
author: Gabriel Sherman
notes: How the epic fight to control the last great media dynasty broke a family and the world.

title: Emotional
author: Leonard Mlodinow
notes: Neuroscience of emotions.

title: Sneaker Wars
author: Barbara Smit
notes: Adidas vs Puma family feud.

title: The Law of Attraction
author: Esther & Jerry Hicks
notes: Foundational LOA text.

title: The Simulation Hypothesis
author: Rizwan Virk
notes: An MIT computer scientist shows why AI, quantum physics, and Eastern mystics agree we are in a video game.

title: What Went Wrong with Capitalism
author: Ruchir Sharma
notes: Economic critique. Recent release (2024).

title: Life After Google
author: George Gilder
notes: Cryptocurrency advocacy.

title: Make It, Don't Fake It
author: Sabrina Horn
notes: Business authenticity guide.

title: The Book of Help
author: Megan Griswold
notes: Self-help memoir.

title: Epic Disruptions
author: Scott D. Anthony
notes: Lessons from disruption and innovation strategy.

title: Hell to Pay
author: Michael Lind
notes: How the suppression of wages is destroying America.

title: Hubris Maximus
author: Faiz Siddiqui
notes: Elon Musk's quest for power, from Tesla to Twitter to government.

title: Every Screen on the Planet
author: Emily Baker-White
notes: The war over TikTok. Tech, politics, and national security collide.

title: The Secret History of the World
author: Mark Booth
notes: Occult/esoteric history.

title: Entrepreneurship for Dummies
author: Kathleen Allen
notes: Basic business startup guide.
